contract.name: v2/awards/funding_rollup.md
contract.description: Returns summed transaction obligations and counts of distinct awarding agencies, funding agencies, and federal accounts for a specific award.

award_id.description: Identifier for the award to roll up.
award_id.constraints: required; accepts strings or integers; leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed; whitespace-only strings resolve to zero totals; rejects floats, booleans, null, arrays, and objects with HTTP 422; extraneous body keys are ignored; unknown ids return zero totals

total_transaction_obligated_amount.description: Sum of transaction obligated amounts for the award; may be negative.

awarding_agency_count.description: Count of distinct awarding agencies linked to the award.

funding_agency_count.description: Count of distinct funding agencies linked to the award.

federal_account_count.description: Count of distinct federal accounts funding the award.

body.detail: Missing value: 'award_id' is a required field
Invalid value in 'award_id'. '123.45' is not a valid type (integer, text)

quirks: Additional body keys such as page, sort, order, and limit are ignored; the response always contains a single aggregate record.
award_id strings are trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace; whitespace-only strings resolve to zero totals while an empty string literal is rejected with HTTP 422.
Integer award_id values are accepted, but floats, booleans, null, arrays, and objects trigger HTTP 422 validation errors.
Unknown award identifiers return HTTP 200 with zero totals instead of signaling an error.
Requests must send application/json; other content types (e.g., text/plain) return HTTP 415.
total_transaction_obligated_amount can be negative for some awards.
risks: Passing an incorrect award_id silently returns zeros, so clients must validate identifiers upstream.
Negative obligation totals can occur and may need handling in downstream analytics.
Clients must send application/json; other content types fail with HTTP 415.
gaps: Did not confirm which award identifier formats (e.g., PIID vs FAIN vs URI) are accepted beyond tested samples.
Maximum tolerated payload size and rate limits were not exercised.

probes.notes: Valid award id returns aggregated totals.
Omitting award_id yields a required field error.
Unknown award id returns zero totals.

mismatches: Docs show page, sort, order, and limit in the request body, but the live endpoint ignores these fields and always returns a single aggregate object.
gaps: Did not confirm which award identifier formats (e.g., PIID vs FAIN vs URI) are accepted beyond tested samples.
Maximum tolerated payload size and rate limits were not exercised.
risks: Passing an incorrect award_id silently returns zeros, so clients must validate identifiers upstream.
Negative obligation totals can occur and may need handling in downstream analytics.
Clients must send application/json; other content types fail with HTTP 415.
